name: first failure is a normal observation, not the end
note: KD2: a failed CuaActionResult is fed forward for recovery; the goal keeps going. The loop only ends on a resolver-emitted done|blocked, an exhausted budget, or an interrupt. (useVoiceCuaController dispatchPlan + continueGoal.)

name: recovery via an alternative call succeeds
note: KD2 recovery: the failing (tool,args) is remembered, but a genuinely different call the resolver tries next still runs. Only a verbatim-repeated failed signature is refused.

ticks.error: click: no actionable element at index 0

name: repeated failed signature stops the loop with blocked
note: KD2 recovery floor: when the resolver re-issues a (tool,args) that already FAILED this goal, the loop refuses to dispatch it and stops with a blocked result instead of looping the dead action to the budget. blockedReasonContains is the exact contract phrase from useVoiceCuaController.

ticks.reason: Stopped: the resolver kept retrying a call that already failed (launch_app).

expected.blockedReasonContains: kept retrying a call that already failed

name: blocked failure carries the post-action window state
note: cuaActionResultSchema: every variant may carry an optional post-action `state` (CuaWindowState). The Rust mapper reports element_count but leaves elements empty (ADR 0005 blocker) — the fixture exercises that exact shape so the Swift decode preserves the count without fabricating elements.
